$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; usesContext extended with queryId + artisanpack/postPreview so the block previews inside a query loop (#483). Attribute schema and supports carried verbatim for parity.

Previews via the lightweight createEntityPlaceholderEdit helper (renders the stamped _resolvedAuthorName when present, otherwise a labelled placeholder). Upstream edit.js is intentionally NOT delegated: it queries the user entity through @wordpress/core-data, which the post editor does not populate, so upstream renders empty. Front-end markup is produced server-side from the stamped _resolvedAuthorName / _resolvedAuthorUrl attributes.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolved* attributes.

Bidirectional core/post-author-name ↔ artisanpack/post-author-name block transforms for the rollout window. Upstream transforms.js converts core/post-author → core/post-author-name (textAlign migration); the equivalent legacy migration for the artisanpack namespace is unnecessary because artisanpack/post-author-name is brand new and has no saved instances in the wild.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit rather than upstream edit.js — the core-data shim does not expose the user entity to the post editor, so the upstream entity-querying edit renders empty. Live in-editor entity values would require expanding the shim (out of scope for the fork).
post-author-name is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolvedAuthorName / _resolvedAuthorUrl attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/post-author-name during the V1 rollout.
Upstream ships a deprecated.js chain migrating legacy textAlign attribute shapes; the fork omits it because the namespace is brand new and has no legacy saved instances.
